Who We Are

About KarmYodha

KarmYodha is dedicated to identifying real gaps in defence and aerospace systems and guiding young innovators to develop practical solutions. Our goal is not just innovation — but meaningful innovation that solves real operational problems faced by defence forces.

We believe that the next generation of engineers and researchers can contribute powerful ideas when they are directed toward the right problems.

Mission

To guide young innovators toward solving real defence challenges by identifying technological gaps and encouraging impactful engineering solutions.

Vision

To build a strong ecosystem of young defence innovators capable of developing breakthrough technologies that strengthen national security.

2+Active Concepts

Research and IP

Our Innovations

Underwater Defence

Passive Sonar Deception System

India's and Asia's first Passive Sonar Deception System concept — designed to mislead enemy sonar detection systems by generating deceptive acoustic signatures and improving underwater survivability.

Aerospace Safety

ALCAS — Auto Locking Canopy Activation System

A mechanical canopy safety mechanism designed for fighter aircraft to improve pilot safety during extreme manoeuvres or impact situations, ensuring rapid and reliable canopy response.

All innovations developed under KarmYodha follow a structured development process. Every concept begins with detailed research, problem analysis, and technical documentation. We focus heavily on theoretical groundwork and patent documentation before moving toward practical development and testing. All innovations under KarmYodha are patented or in the process of patent protection.

Leadership

The Man Behind
The Mission

Aryan Singh
Founder, KarmYodha

Aryan Singh is an 18 year old aerospace engineering student and defence innovator from India. He founded KarmYodha to guide young innovators toward solving real defence technology challenges and developing practical engineering solutions that create meaningful impact.

Young Innovator Program

Join the Mission

KarmYodha invites students and innovators passionate about defence technology, aerospace engineering and problem solving. If you have the drive to build solutions that matter, we want to hear from you.
